_comment: WC2026 Group H, Matchday 2. MD1: Spain 0-0 Cape Verde (embarrassing); Saudi Arabia 1-1 Uruguay. All on 1 point. FanDuel odds Spain -1000 (1.10) / Draw +950 (10.5) / Saudi Arabia +2000 (21.0). Kalshi 89/9/4. Venue Atlanta. Lambdas deliberately a bit BELOW the market: Spain without a true striker and with Yamal managed, against an organized low block.

meta.stakes: Group H with all 4 teams on 1 point. Spain comes off a 0-0 with Cape Verde (couldn't break the block) and needs to win; Saudi Arabia, buoyed after the 1-1 with Uruguay, will play to defend and counter.

home.name: Spain
notes: XI ADJUSTMENT: Yamal STARTS (though he wouldn't play 90) and Dani Olmo comes in for Fabian -> more creative midfield. attack 1.5->1.59, narrowing the gap with the market.
Still a bit BELOW the market (86%): no true striker and coming off a 0-0 with Cape Verde; the difficulty breaking down low blocks persists.
Laporte is DOUBTFUL (knock); if he doesn't make it, Eric Garcia comes in. Barely moves the xG (Eric Garcia also distributes), but it DOES affect his passes prop.

away.name: Saudi Arabia
notes: Organized low block; drew with Uruguay. Concedes volume against Spanish possession but doesn't fall apart easily -> defense 1.31 (not as leaky as a Curacao).
Limited attack on the counter; scored against Uruguay in isolation -> attack 0.65.
Sits deep: cagey match, hence rho -0.07.

cards._referee: Raphael Claus (Brazil), CONFIRMED. Career ~4.1-4.4 YC/match. CONMEBOL referee (tends to book a bit more); discount for the international context but a bump because a deep-sitting Saudi Arabia commits many tactical fouls.

_postmatch.result: Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia (3-0 by the 27th; 21 shots from Spain, Saudi Arabia 0-1 on target)
_postmatch.verdict: My contrarian lean (~4.4 pts below the market) FAILED. Spain swept them aside. The market was right.
_postmatch.lesson: Don't over-correct downward over ONE 0-0 against a low block. An elite team with dominant possession (European champion) ends up breaking down weak blocks; the 0-0 vs Cape Verde was the outlier, not the norm. For elite favorites aligned with a unanimous market (86-89%), respect the market unless there's strong and repeated evidence, not a single match. Had I recalibrated to ~2.9/0.45 (Spain ~86%) it would have been in line.
_postmatch.user_bet: 6-leg combo at odds 10.0, 30 EUR -> cash out 214.29 (+184). Held beyond the EV-optimal cash out at halftime (~80); +variance that paid off; closed well at 85'.
